title: Cheilanthes viridis
taxonomy: Cheilanthes viridis is classified within the family Pteridaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as the maidenhair ferns. This plant belongs to the genus Cheilanthes, which consists of about 70 species of ferns found in various parts of the world. The species Cheilanthes viridis is further classified as a member of the subgenus Cheilanthes. Within the subgenus, it is part of the section Cheilanthes. The classification is based on morphological characteristics such as leaf shape, size, and venation patterns.
uses: Cheilanthes viridis, also known as the green lip fern or green rudder fern, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are thought to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves contain compounds that have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antiseptic properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. In traditional medicine, Cheilanthes viridis is often used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and sore throats. However, further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.

constituents: Cheilanthes viridis is known to contain several bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These active constituents are responsible for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in Cheilanthes viridis have been shown to exhibit significant antioxidant and free radical scavenging activities. Additionally, the plant's phenolic acids have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The saponins present in the plant are known to exhibit hemolytic and molluscicidal activities.
preparations: Cheilanthes viridis is known for its use in herbal preparations, particularly in the treatment of various health issues.
